mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a powerful backup and restore operator for Kubernetes, allowing you to back up cluster resources and persistent volumes. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and migration of applications across clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of storage backends and potential complexity in managing backups and restores.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Velero to manage backups and restores.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct CRDs for the version.

steps.title: Install Velero using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Velero in the specified namespace with the required configuration.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Create a Backup
steps.description:
This command creates a backup of the entire cluster, which can be restored later if needed.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces '*' --wait

```

> This will create a backup named 'my-backup'.

steps.title: Verify Velero Installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Velero server is running and ready to use.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step will remove Velero and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command removes the Velero release.
> This command deletes the Velero namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration and data. This process includes draining the Velero pod, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <velero-pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl uncordon <velero-pod-name>

```

> Replace <velero-pod-name> with the actual pod name.
> This command upgrades Velero to the specified version.
> Replace <velero-pod-name> with the actual pod name to allow scheduling again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified S3 bucket does not exist or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the S3 bucket name and permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Velero server is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Velero server pod may have crashed or failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <velero-pod-name> -n velero' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No backup storage location found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the backup storage location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the backup storage location is set in the Helm install command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Volume snapshot location not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs if the volume snapshot location is not configured.

**Fix:**
Check your Velero configuration and ensure the volume snapshot location is set correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for volume snapshots on AWS, improved CLI commands for backup and restore.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, ensure your S3 bucket policies are updated to allow snapshot access.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
